Tests the Math.asin function to confirm it returns NaN for input -3.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test script that verifies the behavior of the `Math.asin` function in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to check that the result of `Math.asin(-3)` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is the expected outcome since the input value is outside the valid range for the arcsine function. The script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single mathematical operation and its expected result. This test ensures that the `Math.asin` function correctly handles inputs that are not within the domain of the arcsine function.
